ksa_text: Knowledge of the regulatory and compliance landscape governing enterprise data and AI systems, including data protection law, AI-specific regulation, sector-specific obligations, cross-jurisdictional requirements, and regulatory authority oversight mechanisms.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of laws, regulations, and policies related to AI, data security/privacy, and use of publicly procured data for government.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of intelligence laws and regulations

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Knowledge of laws, regulations, and policies related to AI, data security/privacy, and use of publicly procured data for government.
Knowledge of intelligence laws and regulations

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge case — included on balance: knowledge contains GOV-relevant signals ("regulatory   :compliance|requirement|obligation|framework|reporting", "data protection   :law|regulation|compliance|officer|policy|regim") within a Security & Privacy context (affinity 0.55). STRM composite is strong (0.5055). A director-level Data Governance Director would encounter this competency in governance practice.

pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: External validation strong: 3 job-posting-level and 0 certification-level matches (CDMP, DAMA, IAPP, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001). Competency appears regularly in Data Governance Director role descriptions and/or governance certification curricula.

decision_rationale: Edge case — included on balance: knowledge contains GOV-relevant signals ("regulatory   :compliance|requirement|obligation|framework|reporting", "data protection   :law|regulation|compliance|officer|policy|regim") within a Security & Privacy context (affinity 0.55). STRM composite is strong (0.5055). A director-level Data Governance Director would encounter this competency in governance practice.
